title: Akwin
content:

{{Quote|The sea is our home, and the land is home of the Lazerians. Their problems are not ours and our problems are not theirs. If legends we are to them, then legends we shall be, at least until their minds expand to the point where they can begin looking beyond their silly stories.|The Akwin's belief regarding contact with the Lazerians|<ref name="TSoK" />}}
The '''Akwin''' were an [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibious]] [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]] that resided in the [[Father Ocean|oceans]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lazerian IV]]. Though Akwin society was advanced, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] population of Lazerian IV—the [[Lazerian (people)|Lazerians]]—believed the Akwin to be [[Ocean/Legends|sea]] monsters, which convinced the oceanic beings to refrain from contacting their land-dwelling counterparts. The Akwin's isolationist attitude continued into the [[Post-Imperial era/Legends|New Republic era]]. As the Lazerians continued to react poorly to sightings of the Akwin, the latter concluded that their land-dwelling neighbors were still not ready to meet them formally. Despite their continued isolationism, the Akwin had multiple interactions with some travelers who visited Lazerian IV.

The Akwin utilized a degree of industry in the form of [[Factory/Legends|manufacturing plants]] and [[Ore/Legends|ore]] [[Mining/Legends|mines]], though their pride for Lazerian IV led to restraint from polluting their planet. For the same reason, they were frustrated by the pollution caused by the Lazerians on the surface. Akwin utilized technology such as [[Akwin Net Gun|net guns]] and [[Akwin speeder|speeders]] and practiced an art called [[Coral-shaping|coral-sculpting]]. The Akwin lived in multiple independent [[City-state/Legends|city-states]] ruled by a [[King/Legends|king]] or [[Queen/Legends|queen]], built on the ocean floor.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:AkwinDonut.png|thumb|left|200px|An Akwin]]
An [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Mammal/Legends|mammalian]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], the Akwin were [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibious]], allowing them to reside on land and underwater. Their bodies had six [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], with the first pair at their shoulders acting as arms. Each of their arms ended in a hand with three digits. The middle set of limbs were flippers resembling arms, which Akwin used to steer themselves through the water. Their third and final pair of limbs acted as the Akwin's legs, which ended in webbed, two-toed feet that allowed them to stand and locomote on land. The mammals also had a tail and fins on their backs, a set of gills below their jaws, and acute hearing.<ref name="TSoK" />

An Akwin's body was tough and muscular, which allowed them to tolerate the pressure of the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans']] depths. They had elongated skulls with a broad [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], a pair of [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] adjusted for [[Local day/Legends|night]] vision, and two [[Nose/Legends|blowholes]]. Cilia grew from their heads and faces and could often be mistaken for [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. This cilia was typically [[Color/Legends|white]], silver, green, blue, or black in color. An elongated crest extended from the back of their heads and had two holes in its end. The Akwin were [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]], primarily consuming small sea creatures, but also on marine [[Plant/Legends|plant life]]. They ranged anywhere from 1.5 to 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height<ref name="TSoK" /> and had blue-green skin.<ref name="Alien Encounters" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|In my opinion, they have a long way to go before they allow themselves to shake loose of their backward ideas.|Akwin scout B'dasssa, regarding the Lazerians|<ref name="TSoK" />}}
[[File:AkwinOceanVilardi.png|thumb|right|300px|The Akwin resided in the depths of Lazerian IV's oceans]]
Due to the discovery by the [[Lazerian (people)|Lazerians]]—the Akwin's [[Human/Legends|Human]] neighbors—of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel, their interest was focused primarily on exploring [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and the [[Lazerian system/Legends|Lazerian system]]. As a result, the Akwin remained almost entirely undisturbed by the Lazerians, as the latter believed them to be sea monsters. The presence of [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]] that blocked [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] scans of the oceans also kept their people hidden. Although the Akwin's technology was on par with their Human neighbors, they were not interested in space travel. They had made significant progress in perfecting water-resistant technology and developed their own fast-moving [[Akwin speeder|variant]] of [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] with two sub-variations: one that could move both in water and in the air and one that could move both in water and on land.<ref name="TSoK" />

The Akwin also utilized [[Factory/Legends|manufacturing plants]] and [[Ore/Legends|ore]] [[Mine/Legends|mines]], though they took great care to prevent polluting their [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lazerian IV]]'s environment, as they were proud of their world. Likewise, excessive pollution from their surface-dwelling neighbors infuriated them and had the potential to cause the Akwin to reveal themselves to the Lazerians. Despite their civilized and advanced nature, they could be rather mischievous. They were often equipped with underwater equivalents to many surface-dweller technologies, including [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], [[Comlink/Legends|comlinks]], and [[Akwin Net Gun]]s.<ref name="TSoK" />

[[File:RepublicResistanceLazerianIV.png|thumb|left|200px|The Akwin discouraged contact with the Lazerians (pictured), their Human neighbors.]]
Although the Akwin could live on land and in the sea, they spent most of their [[Time/Legends|time]] in Lazerian IV's oceans—[[Caz Ocean]] and [[Dox Ocean]]—which they referred to together as "[[Father Ocean]]." The species raised the creatures that comprised their diet on underwater "ranches," while the plant life they ate was grown on special underwater farms. The Akwin were a peaceful people who lived in large [[City/Legends|cities]] built on the ocean floor, which operated as independent [[City-state/Legends|city-states]], each ruled by its own [[King/Legends|king]] or [[Queen/Legends|queen]]. They enjoyed [[Music/Legends|music]] as well as the art of [[Coral-shaping|coral-sculpting]]. The Akwin communicated through both [[Language/Legends|spoken words]] and gestures. Additionally, they kept historical records dating back to their savage ancestors, who lived [[Millennium/Legends|thousands]] of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before them.<ref name="TSoK" />

Contact with the Lazerians was greatly discouraged by the Akwin, who believed both were content with their homes and that it should remain that way. To some extent, the Akwin believed themselves superior to their counterparts, as they were not confined only to the soil, which was further reinforced by them being far more aware of Lazerian civilization than the opposite. What little knowledge the Lazerians did have of the Akwin was often relegated to stories or legends and usually dismissed as mirages or a product of [[Alcohol/Legends|inebriation]]. The idea that their people were a myth was promoted by the Akwin, who believed the Lazerians were not yet prepared to share their planet with another advanced civilization. The Akwin were also aware of a [[ruined outpost]] on the planet's surface.<ref name="TSoK" />

==History==
===Life with Lazerians===
During the reign of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Akwin once witnessed a [[Battle on Lazerian IV|battle]] at the ruined outpost involving [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]—whom they regarded as strangers—using their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] to cut through objects easily. Shortly after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]<ref name="TSoK" /> at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> a [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] party of Akwin—including the scout [[B'dasssa]]—saw the Lazerian [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Lazeria City]], ensuring they remained unseen. They listened as a group of [[Lazerian army]] [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] discussed the demise of Palpatine and their dissatisfaction with change.<ref name="TSoK" />

As the scouting party continued eavesdropping, they failed to notice the arrival of a second [[Army/Legends|army]] group. The newly arrived troopers noticed the Akwin scouts and began shouting before opening fire, missing as the Akwin fled. A few [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] later, B'dasssa instructed their party to remain in the depths while they returned to investigate further. They were surprised to learn that the troopers believed the Akwin to be a [[Hallucination/Legends|hallucination]] of giant creatures. Although the incident convinced B'dasssa that the Lazerians were not yet prepared for contact with the Akwin, they also confessed that they were curious about the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] fate.<ref name="TSoK" />

By the time following the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|formation]]<ref name="TSoK" /> in 4 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> the Akwin had become aware of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. They considered it the natural result of other species' immaturity, though it was possible for their view to shift in favor of the New Republic had they encountered a member of the [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] or [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] species—the consequences of which would be severe due to the Lazerians' pro-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] stance.<ref name="TSoK" />

===Travelers on Lazerian IV===
[[File:Crystal_Valley.jpg|thumb|right|A scouting party of Akwin witnessed a squad of the King's Own Guard marching toward the Crystal Valley (pictured).]]
Following the rise of the New Republic during the [[Post-Imperial era/Legends|New Republic era]], a [[Unidentified trader (Lazerian IV)|trader]] took a [[Hologram/Legends|holoimage]] of an [[Unidentified Akwin|Akwin]] on an island near the continent [[Laz]]. He later went to a [[Cantina/Legends|bar]], where he attempted to inform other patrons of what he believed to be a sea creature. Most patrons, however, brushed him off. Unbeknownst to the traveler, the Akwin whose image he had taken informed his friends of the incident.<ref name="TSoK" />

{{RPGseed}}
One group of travelers believed the trader and learned of its probable location on the island after buying him some drinks. A [[Unidentified marine biologist|marine biologist]] under the employ of the Galactic Empire had also overheard the conversation and departed for the island, interested. After the trader himself left, he was harassed by a group of [[Cloak/Legends|cloaked]] Akwin—including the individual whose image he took—who stole the holoimage from him without injuring him. When the band of travelers tried to intervene, the Akwin attempted to subdue them, again taking care not to cause injury. The travelers later left to investigate the island themselves but found themselves to be the Akwin's unwilling guests.<ref name="TSoK" />
{{Endgame}}

Another Akwin scouting party had seen the [[King's Own Guard]] marching into the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]]. What the Akwin did not know, however, was that the King's Own Guard had been bound for the [[Crystal Valley (Lazerian IV)|Crystal Valley]], intending to harvest [[Empeth Crystal]]s that were about to "bloom."<ref name="TSoK" />

{{RPGseed}}
When another group of travelers managed to befriend the Akwin, they were informed of the King's Own Guard squad in the mountains.<ref name="TSoK" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Akwin were created by [[John Terra]] for [[Twin Stars of Kira (sourcebook)|''Twin Stars of Kira'']], a [[1993]] supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. ''Twin Stars of Kira'' uses "Akwins" as the plural form of their name.<ref name="TSoK" /> However, the [[1998]] releases ''[[Alien Encounters]]'' and ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="Alien Encounters" /><ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> as well as the [[2008]] reference work ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|247|Lazerian IV}}</ref> utilize the plural form "Akwin." As the three aforementioned publications use the plural "Akwin" more frequently, and these sources are more recent, this article assumes it is the correct form.<ref name="Alien Encounters" /><ref name="SWE" /><ref name="CSWE" />
